_note: Port webcam directory. Each entry points to a port in ports.json via `portCode` (UN/LOCODE). Three source types are supported: `youtube` (iframe embed via videoId or channelId for latest-live), `hls` (direct .m3u8 URL rendered with hls.js), and `image` (single URL that can be refreshed on an interval). Camera location is [lon, lat] and MAY differ from the port centroid — the webcam icon is placed at this spot on the globe. To swap in a real stream: change `type` + `src` (+ `videoId` or `channelId` for youtube) while keeping `id` + `portCode`.

webcams.title: Singapore — Tuas Megaport
webcams.description: World's second-busiest transshipment hub. Tuas phased opening 2022-2040 consolidating all of Singapore's container ops.

webcams.title: Rotterdam — Europoort / Maasvlakte
webcams.description: Europe's largest port; Maasvlakte 2 is fully automated with AGV yards and remote-operated STS cranes.

webcams.title: Los Angeles — Pier 400
webcams.description: Largest US container port. Pier 400 (APM Terminals) handles the majority of Asia-West Coast throughput.

webcams.title: Hamburg — Waltershof Terminal
webcams.description: Germany's largest seaport, 110 km inland on the Elbe. HHLA Burchardkai has been semi-automated since 2011.

webcams.title: Shanghai — Yangshan Deep-Water Port
webcams.description: World's busiest container port since 2010. Phase IV (opened 2017) is the largest fully-automated terminal on Earth.

webcams.title: Hong Kong — Kwai Tsing Terminals
webcams.description: Eight terminals along Kwai Chung and Tsing Yi. Declining throughput as Shenzhen and Guangzhou overtake.

webcams.title: Busan — New Port (Sinhang)
webcams.description: Transpacific transshipment hub; Sinhang handles most of Busan's 23M TEU / year throughput.

webcams.title: Antwerp — Deurganck Dock
webcams.description: Europe's second-largest port. Deurganckdok handles the largest call sizes on the Scheldt.

webcams.title: Valencia — Muelle Príncipe Felipe
webcams.description: Western Mediterranean's busiest container port; major Maersk and MSC calls.

webcams.title: Panama — Gatun Locks
webcams.description: Northern entrance of the Panama Canal. 2016 Neopanamax locks added a third, larger lane.

webcams.title: Suez Canal — Port Said North
webcams.description: Northern entrance of the Suez Canal; SCVTS radar and pilot station.

webcams.title: Jebel Ali — DP World Terminal 3
webcams.description: Largest man-made harbor in the world. DP World's flagship operation on the Persian Gulf.

webcams.title: Seattle–Tacoma — Terminal 5
webcams.description: PNW gateway for Alaska and Transpacific services. T5 reopened 2022 after $500M modernization.

webcams.title: New York / NJ — APM Terminal Elizabeth
webcams.description: East Coast's busiest container port. Bayonne Bridge raised in 2017 to clear Neopanamax vessels.

webcams.title: Kobe — Rokko Island
webcams.description: Recovered to ~50% of pre-1995-earthquake volume; major Japanese transshipment hub on the Seto Inland Sea.

webcams.title: Durban — Pier 2 Container Terminal
webcams.description: Busiest port in Sub-Saharan Africa. Chronic congestion; ~70% of SA container volume.

webcams.title: Santos — Margem Direita
webcams.description: Largest port in Latin America; handles ~30% of Brazilian international trade.

webcams.title: Piraeus — Container Terminal (COSCO)
webcams.description: COSCO majority stake since 2016. Eastern Mediterranean's fastest-growing box port.

webcams.title: Genoa — PSA Genova Pra'
webcams.description: Italy's busiest container port. Morandi Bridge collapse in 2018 temporarily severed road access.

webcams.title: Vancouver — Roberts Bank / Deltaport
webcams.description: Canada's largest port. Deltaport handles the majority of transpacific rail-out containers.
